Amelia (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $14,279,575Details
International Box Office $5,476,502
Worldwide Box Office $19,756,077
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,495,991
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $420,008
Total $6,915,999
Further financial details...

Synopsis

From the time she first sits in the pilot’s seat, aviatrix Amelia Earhart feels destined to achieve great things. Beginning in 1928, she sets a number of aviation milestones, including numerous speed and altitude records, and was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic. In 1937, Amelia undertakes her greatest challenge of all: to become the first woman to circumnavigate the globe by airplane.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$3,904,047 (27.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.66 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:72.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:820 opening theaters/1,070 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $21,529,467

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 23rd, 2009 (Wide) by Fox Searchlight
Video Release: February 2nd, 2010 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some sensuality, language, thematic elements and smoking
Running Time: 111 minutes
Plot point: Air Race, Biography, Woman in a Male Dominated Profession, World Record Attempt
Social setting: In a Plane
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1930s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Avalon Studio Productions
Production Countries: United States, Canada
Languages: English

Synopsis

From the time she first sits in the pilot’s seat, aviatrix Amelia Earhart feels destined to achieve great things. Beginning in 1928, she sets a number of aviation milestones, including numerous speed and altitude records, and was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic. In 1937, Amelia undertakes her greatest challenge of all: to become the first woman to circumnavigate the globe by airplane.
